title: Asking a big favor
setting: Your laptop broke right before a deadline. You call Roberto to ask for serious help.

options.es: Roberto, ¿te puedo pedir un favor grande?
options.en: Roberto, can I ask you a big favor?
options.feedback: Signaling upfront that it's a big ask is courteous — it lets him prepare.
Demanding, not asking. A big favor needs a softer, more respectful opener.
Polite, though a touch over-apologetic. Friends don't need so much hedging.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.en: Of course, tell me. What do you need?

options.es: ¿Me podrías prestar tu laptop unos días?
options.en: Could you lend me your laptop for a few days?
options.feedback: 'Podrías' (could you) is the soft, polite conditional — ideal for a real favor.
Direct command — fine between close friends, but 'podrías' lands softer for a big ask.
'Give me' sounds like you're keeping it! And it's too demanding. Ask to borrow, gently.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Uy, la uso para el trabajo... ¿para cuándo la necesitás?
steps.en: Oof, I use it for work... when do you need it?

options.es: Solo hasta el viernes. Te la cuido bien.
options.en: Just until Friday. I'll take good care of it.
options.feedback: Specific timeframe plus reassurance — you're addressing his exact worry. Excellent.
Vague timing on someone's work computer? That'll make him hesitate. Be precise.

options.es: Entiendo si no podés, no hay problema.
options.en: I understand if you can't, no problem.
options.feedback: Gracious — giving him an out is kind, though you can still make your case first.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Está bien, confío en vos. Pasá por ella.
steps.en: Alright, I trust you. Come get it.

options.es: De verdad te lo agradezco un montón.
options.en: I really appreciate it a ton.
options.feedback: Heartfelt thanks for a real favor — and it honors the trust he just showed.

options.en: Thanks, brother. I owe you one.
options.feedback: 'Te debo una' (I owe you one) is perfect — warm and acknowledges the favor.
A bit flat for someone lending you their work laptop. A real thanks fits better.
